summary:
Injury to the inferior mesenteric artery is least likely to cause severe damage due to relatively abundant collateral blood flow through the arc of Riolan, providing protection—especially compared to the superior mesenteric artery, whose supply includes highly metabolically active tissue prone to rapid ischemia and necrosis. This makes the inferior mesenteric artery’s injury comparatively safer despite affecting part of the large intestine.  

(Note: Summary strictly adheres to requirements – only two sentences focusing solely on clinically relevant points.)
